enonce.texte: Exercise 1: Match the word to its definition. Draw a line or write the letter next to the number.

questions.texte: Match each term with the correct definition.

sousQuestions.texte: balanced diet
sousQuestions.reponse: d. Eating a variety of foods to get the right nutrients

sousQuestions.texte: expiry date
sousQuestions.reponse: e. The last day a food product is safe to eat

sousQuestions.texte: perishable
sousQuestions.reponse: a. Food that can go bad quickly (e.g., fish, milk)

sousQuestions.texte: non-perishable
sousQuestions.reponse: c. Food that lasts a long time (e.g., rice, cans)

sousQuestions.texte: menu
sousQuestions.reponse: b. A list of food and drinks available in a restaurant

sousQuestions.texte: meal
sousQuestions.reponse: f. An occasion of eating, like breakfast, lunch, or dinner

sousQuestions.texte: eru
sousQuestions.reponse: h. A green leafy vegetable often cooked with waterleaf and palm oil

sousQuestions.texte: plantain
sousQuestions.reponse: g. A starchy fruit similar to banana, eaten fried, boiled, or roasted

enonce.texte:
Exercise 2: Complete the sentences. Choose the correct quantifier from the box. Some quantifiers may be used more than once.

some, any, much, many, a little, a few, both, neither, no

questions.texte: Fill in each blank with the appropriate quantifier.
questions.reponse: 2.1 some, 2.2 many, 2.3 much, 2.4 a few, 2.5 a little, 2.6 both, 2.7 neither, 2.8 any, 2.9 no

sousQuestions.texte: There is ______ fish and chicken on the menu tonight.
sousQuestions.reponse: some

sousQuestions.texte: How ______ vegetables do you eat every day?
sousQuestions.reponse: many

sousQuestions.texte: I don't have ______ time to cook a big meal.
sousQuestions.reponse: much

sousQuestions.texte: She added ______ drops of palm oil to the eru.
sousQuestions.reponse: a few

sousQuestions.texte: Would you like ______ more water with your plantain?
sousQuestions.reponse: a little

sousQuestions.texte: ______ rice and beans are common staple foods in Cameroon.
sousQuestions.reponse: Both

sousQuestions.texte: ______ fish nor chicken is left; we only have vegetables.
sousQuestions.reponse: Neither

sousQuestions.texte: Is there ______ bread to go with the meal?
sousQuestions.reponse: any

sousQuestions.texte: There are ______ more non-perishable items on the shelf.
sousQuestions.reponse: no

enonce.texte:
Exercise 3: Real-life problem solving. Read the situation and answer the questions.

You are at a supermarket in Yaoundé. You pick up a bag of rice. On the label, you see: "Best before: 15/03/2025. Non-perishable. Store in a cool, dry place." Today is 10/04/2026.

questions.texte: Analyze the label and make a decision.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify: Is the rice perishable or non-perishable? Explain your answer using a word from Lesson 9.
sousQuestions.reponse: Non-perishable, because the label says 'Non-perishable' and rice lasts a long time without spoiling.

sousQuestions.texte: Calculate: Is the expiry date passed? Justify your answer.
sousQuestions.reponse: Yes, the expiry date (15/03/2025) is passed because today is 10/04/2026, which is more than one year later.

sousQuestions.texte: Decide: Would you buy this rice? Give one reason for your decision using vocabulary from the lesson.
sousQuestions.reponse: No, I would not buy it because the expiry date is passed, so the rice might not be safe to eat even if it is non-perishable.

enonce.texte: Exercise 4: Design a meal. You are asked to plan one balanced meal for a family of four in Cameroon. Use the vocabulary from Lesson 9.

questions.texte: Create a menu and justify your choices.

sousQuestions.texte: List three foods from Lesson 9 that you will include in the meal. (Example: rice, fish, vegetables)
sousQuestions.reponse: Any three from: rice, eru, plantain, fish, chicken, vegetables, bread. Example: plantain, fish, vegetables.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain why your meal is a 'balanced diet' using two complete sentences.
sousQuestions.reponse: This meal is balanced because it contains carbohydrates from plantain, protein from fish, and vitamins from vegetables. A balanced diet gives the body all the nutrients it needs to stay healthy.

sousQuestions.texte: Recommend one perishable and one non-perishable item from the lesson that you would buy for this meal. Justify your choice for each.
sousQuestions.reponse: Perishable: fish (must be eaten quickly or frozen). Non-perishable: rice (can be stored for months). I choose fish for protein and rice for energy and long storage.
